name: Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike
name_original: Barbaste et le Moulin d'Henri IV – boucle
description: Hike the 9.9-mile Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan loop, a moderate trail winding through diverse landscapes and past historic Henry IV's Mil
region_intro: Hiking around Barbaste offers diverse natural landscapes in southwestern France, characterized by extensive forests covering much of its territory. The region features a mix of woodlands, open fields, and traditional architecture, situated at the confluence of the Pays d'Albret and the Landes forest. The Gélise river and the Béas stream flow through the area, contributing to its verdant environment and providing opportunities for waterside walks. Historical landmarks like the Moulin des Tours and the Roman Bridge are integrated into the local trail network. Best hiking trails around Barbaste The most popular hiking route is Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike, a 9.9 miles (15.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects several local hamlets and follows the course of the Béas stream. Another top favourite among local hikers is Moulin des Tours de Barbaste – Barbaste Roman Bridge loop from Cauderoue, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the historic Moulin des Tours and the Roman Bridge along the Gélise river. Local hikers also love the Laundry loop from Las Mourelles, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through woodlands and open fields, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Barbaste is defined by extensive forests, the Gélise river, and historical landmarks such as the Moulin des Tours. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Barbaste are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 75 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Barbaste's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Barbaste?
A: Barbaste offers a diverse network of over two dozen hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 8 easy trails and 16 moderate excursions, ensuring there's something for everyone to explore the region's forests and riverbanks.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Barbaste?
A: Hiking in Barbaste immerses you in a landscape dominated by extensive forests, which cover nearly 70% of the area. You'll encounter a mix of pine, oak, and cork oak trees, along with open fields and traditional colombage houses. The Gélise river and the Béas stream also contribute to the verdant scenery, offering pleasant waterside walks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Barbaste?
A: Yes, Barbaste has several easy trails suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41008399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laundry loop from Las Mourelles&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7 km route through woodlands and fields, typically completed in under two hours. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15103706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Henry IV's Mill – Barbaste Roman Bridge loop from Barbaste&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.7 km and offers historical sights.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover along the hiking routes?
A: Barbaste's hiking trails often incorporate significant historical landmarks. A prominent feature is the &lt;strong&gt;Moulin des Tours de Barbaste&lt;/strong&gt; (Henri IV Mill), a fortified 13th-century mill, and the adjacent 12th-century &lt;strong&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/strong&gt;. You can explore these sites on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26413304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin des Tours de Barbaste – Barbaste Roman Bridge loop from Cauderoue&lt;/a&gt;. For more historical exploration, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4718016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nérac Old Town and Château d'Henri IV&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415519" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Christopher's Church in Vianne&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Barbaste?
A: Many of Barbaste's hiking trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26413304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin des Tours de Barbaste – Barbaste Roman Bridge loop from Cauderoue&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41008399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laundry loop from Las Mourelles&lt;/a&gt;. These loops offer convenient ways to experience the local scenery and attractions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Barbaste?
A: The hiking trails in Barbaste are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 75 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the quiet woodlands, the scenic paths along the Gélise river, and the well-maintained routes that lead to historical sites like the Moulin des Tours.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Barbaste?
A: Barbaste is generally dog-friendly, with many trails suitable for walks with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in forested areas or near livestock, and to respect local regulations regarding pet access in specific parks or nature reserves. Always carry waste bags and ensure your dog is well-behaved around other hikers and wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Barbaste?
A: The best time to hike in Barbaste is typically during the spring and autumn months. Spring offers pleasant temperatures and blooming flora, while autumn provides cooler weather and beautiful fall foliage. Summer can be warm, but the extensive forests offer welcome shade on many trails. Winter hiking is also possible, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any other outdoor activities available in Barbaste besides hiking?
A: Yes, Barbaste is a recognized 'Station Verte' town, offering a variety of outdoor activities. The Cap Cauderoue Leisure Park is a hub for enthusiasts, providing options like tree climbing, mountain biking, horse riding, and even barefoot walking paths. Canoeing on the Gélise river is also a popular choice for those looking to enjoy the water.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails in Barbaste?
A: Parking is generally available at common starting points for trails, such as near the Cap Cauderoue Leisure Park or in the village of Barbaste itself. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26413304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin des Tours de Barbaste – Barbaste Roman Bridge loop from Cauderoue&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking in the vicinity of Cauderoue or Moulin Neuf, which serve as access points to the trail network.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the Gélise river?
A: Yes, the Gélise river is a prominent natural feature in Barbaste, and several hiking trails incorporate its banks. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26413304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moulin des Tours de Barbaste – Barbaste Roman Bridge loop from Cauderoue&lt;/a&gt; offer scenic views along the river, particularly around the historic Moulin des Tours and the Roman Bridge. The riverbanks also provide pleasant spots for picnics and enjoying the waterside environment.
tips:
The mill is located on the northwest side of the municipality of Nérac, in contact with the municipalities of Lavardac and Barbaste . It made it possible to control the passages on the bridge of Barbaste, which is 20 m downstream from the highest tower of the mill. This bridge already existed in 1259 because it is mentioned in the homage Ysarn de Sainte-Marise to the Count of Toulouse for all the land he owned in Agenais.

The mill has a sturdy appearance. The central body has a square plan. It is bordered by four towers also of square plan.

The mill consists of six floors above the floor of the mill wheels.

Height of the towers: 29 m.  (for the highest), 26 m. and 27 m. for the others.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike?
faqs.answer: The hike typically starts in Barbaste. You should be able to find parking within the town, which is nestled between the Gélise river, hills, and forests.

faqs.question: Is the Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near roads, and always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. However, consider that some sections involve sandy paths, which might be more tiring in hotter weather. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a varied terrain, including paved alleys, unpaved and grassy tracks, and significant stretches of sandy paths, particularly within pine forests. There are also wooden footbridges over streams and crossings over various roads.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike?
faqs.answer: This hike is rated as moderate. While some sources might classify it differently, its distance of nearly 16 km, elevation gain of around 250 meters, and the presence of tiring sandy sections suggest a good level of fitness is recommended.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or sights will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: A prominent feature is the historic Henry IV's Mill (Moulin des Tours Henri IV), the largest fortified mill in France, often accompanied by a medieval stone bridge. You'll also pass through the charming villages of Béas and Lausseignan, each with historic churches.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike is a loop trail, meaning you'll end up back near your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Barbaste, la randonnée de Béas et Lausseignan - 15.5 km, La Randonnée des Bastides et Châteaux en Albret - 21.4 km, and EV3 France 43, Buzet-sur-Baïse - Mézin.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Barbaste, as the starting point, offers some local amenities. You may find cafes or other facilities within the village before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including sandy and unpaved sections, sturdy hiking shoes are highly recommended. Bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days, and consider sun protection. Layered clothing is always a good idea.

faqs.question: Is the Barbaste, Béas, and Lausseignan Hike suitable for families?
faqs.answer: While scenic, the hike's length (nearly 16 km) and the presence of tiring sandy paths might make it challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to longer distances. It's best suited for families with older children who have some hiking experience.
